question: Tuning in the ATIS, you hear winds of 300° at 13 knots, gusting to 21 knots. What is the steady headwind component on runway 35R?

question: You plan to use a nice and shallow 2.5° descent all the way to your destination. At an altitude of 35,000 feet with a landing elevation of 13 feet, how far out do you need to start your descent with an average ground speed of 358 knots?

(Using the information from the previous question) Unfortunately, ATC keeps you at 35,000 longer than you would like. They allow you to start your descent just 99 nm from your destination. You start down at a 4° angle to recapture your path. How long before you are back on your desired 2.5° glide path?
choice_b: Approximately 44 nautical miles from the destination
choice_d: Just around 63 statute miles from the destination
correct_choice: Approximately 44 nautical miles from the destination

question: A Navy aircraft tracking north is ready to return to land and the pilot must decide which of 3 nearby stationary ships to return to. One ship (Ship B) is to the right, directly abeam, 166 NM away. Ship A is in front of Ship B and Ship C is behind it. The 3 ships are equally spaced, in a staggered line, with 106.7 NM between ships. Ship C is at a magnetic bearing of 040° to Ship B and Ship A. From the aircraft, Ship A is a 071° course and Ship C is a 130° course. The winds aloft are 180° at 40 knots. The aircraft TAS is 415 knots. How far away is Ship C from the aircraft?
(Using information from the previous question) Which ship will the pilot return to in the fastest time?
choice_d: Ship B or C, the time to reach the ships are equal

question: You are flying along in cruise when your friend decides he needs to use the bathroom.
